highlights.text: I wrote on Monday at 4pm that: “Bear case Monday: Shorter-term, ES can dip on the failure of 6453”.
The subsequent recovery of that zone typically sees a parabolic squeeze and my lean was we would see that Thursday.
I wrote at 4pm Wednesday : “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
When it sells, it is always vertical, and “elevator down”.
This was the “elevator down leg.” After a vertical leg lower like this though, ES always sees an equally vicious snap back rally.
This snap back rally is caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Failed Breakdowns are how large institutions accumulate.
You can think of this as a rubber band being pulled back (the elevator down) then released (the short squeeze).

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the monster Failed Breakdown we saw on Tuesday above 6384 that caused this 100+ point squeeze (you need to know this, because we will see this setup daily if a bear market comes).

post_title: SPX Makes An All Time High, Then Failed. Will Today's Dip Get Bought? September 8th Plan
